name: First Heritage Bank
description_short: Independent FDIC-insured community bank serving Southwest Iowa since 1873, offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards with Kasasa-branded reward accounts.
description_long:
First Heritage Bank is an independent community bank headquartered in Southwest Iowa that has been operating since 1873. The bank serves customers in Shenandoah and Farragut, Iowa, positioning itself as a locally-owned alternative to larger megabanks. As an FDIC-insured institution backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, deposits are protected up to applicable limits.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. On the deposit side, they provide multiple checking accounts (including Kasasa Cash Checking with 4.00% APY on balances up to $15,000), savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, IRAs, and HSAs. Their lending products include personal loans, auto loans, home mortgages, home equity lines of credit, home construction and improvement loans, business loans, agriculture loans, commercial real estate loans, equipment financing, SBA loans, and business lines of credit. They also issue personal and business credit cards, including a Kasasa Cash Back card offering 3.00% cash back on debit card purchases.

First Heritage Bank distinguishes itself through partnership with Kasasa, a fintech platform that enables community banks to offer competitive reward rates on checking and savings accounts. The bank emphasizes local decision-making, community involvement, and personalized service as an alternative to national megabanks. They highlight 147 years of serving local business owners and position themselves as committed to enriching their community. The website notes that as an independent institution, they have "the freedom that comes with ownership."

As a regional community bank, First Heritage Bank is best suited for customers in their service areas seeking relationship banking and competitive deposit rates through Kasasa accounts. However, their geographic footprint is limited to two Iowa towns, which significantly restricts accessibility for most consumers. The high APY rates on Kasasa accounts typically require meeting specific qualification criteria (direct deposits, debit card usage thresholds), and rates drop substantially if qualifications aren't met (0.05% APY on Kasasa Cash). Their loan and credit card products appear standard for community banks but lack detailed rate and fee information on their website.
services: Free Kasasa Cash Checking with 4.00% APY on balances up to $15,000
Free Kasasa Cash Back Checking with 3.00% cash back on debit card purchases
Home Construction, Improvement, and Equity Loans
Business Loans, Lines of Credit, and SBA Loans
Commercial Real Estate and Equipment Loans
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government full faith and credit
Kasasa Cash checking account offers 4.00% APY on balances up to $15,000 for qualified customers
147 years of local banking history and community presence since 1873
Comprehensive product suite including personal and business checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards
Digital banking platform available 24/7 for account management, balance viewing, and payment scheduling
Specialized agriculture loan programs for seed, feed, equipment, and irrigation financing
SBA loan programs available for small business financing
Kasasa Cash Back credit card offers up to $9 cash back per month on debit card purchases
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Shenandoah and Farragut, Iowa only—not accessible to most consumers
Kasasa account high APY rates (4.00%) only apply if qualification requirements are met; drops to 0.05% APY if requirements aren't met
Website lacks specific interest rates and fee information for most loan products, mortgages, and credit cards
No mention of online account opening or digital onboarding capabilities for new customers
Limited product comparison tools or rate calculators visible on primary pages
best_for: Residents of Shenandoah or Farragut, Iowa seeking relationship banking with a locally-owned community bank
Small business owners and agricultural operations in Southwest Iowa needing specialized lending and local decision-making
Customers who qualify for Kasasa account requirements (direct deposits, debit card usage) and can access 4.00% APY checking accounts
Homebuyers in their service area seeking mortgage and home equity lending from a local lender
diagnosis: First Heritage Bank is best for residents of Shenandoah or Farragut, Iowa who value local banking relationships and qualify for Kasasa reward account requirements. The primary limitation is severe geographic restriction to two small Iowa towns, making this institution inaccessible to most consumers; those outside the service area should pursue banking options with broader accessibility or online availability.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
